content.full_text:
RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
CENTRAL BANK TOWER, SUITE 1400
SOUTH COAST PLAZA TOWN CENTER
611 ANTON BOULEVARD
POST OFFICE BOX 1950
COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92628-9990

TELEPHONE
(714) 641-5100

TELECOPIER (714) 546-9035

October 3, 1990


Luisa Menchaca, Esq.
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804-0807

Re:  Conflict Issues Concerning
     Acquisition of Automobile

Dear Luisa:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm our telephone
conversation on September 28, 1990.

     In that conversation I asked you for your thoughts on
conflict of interest issues which might arise in my
contemplated purchase of a new car. I told you that I was
general counsel to a redevelopment agency and attorney
of the same city. (Although I am therefore a public
official, I am not an employee since my law firm serves in
this capacity by contract). I have been involved over the
last six years in attempting to develop an auto center in my
city. These efforts have been successful, and in the last
two years, we have been negotiating with seven different auto
dealers to develop some 20 dealerships. We have now
completed negotiating four of the seven transactions, and one
of the four dealerships is now in construction with
construction of two others to commence shortly.

     I would like to buy a new car. I would like to buy it
from one of our new dealers who I understand can handle the
transaction in such a manner that my city will be credited
with the sales tax. However, I would like to handle the
transaction in such a manner so that I will not be barred in
the future from negotiating on behalf of the redevelopment

                                                           -1-


RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS


LUISA MENCHACA, ESQ.
OCTOBER 3, 1990
RE: CONFLICT ISSUES CONCERNING
    ACQUISITION OF AUTOMOBILE


agency with any of these auto dealers.  Even for the
transactions where the negotiations have all been completed,
there is the possibility that in the future problems may
arise over issuance of building permits and other matters
relating to the development that would need my participation
as agency counsel or city attorney.  Therefore, before
discussing with any of these dealers the purchase of an
automobile, I wanted to find out from the FPPC how the matter
should be handled to avoid my having to abstain from
participating in representing the city and redevelopment
agency in future matters involving these auto dealers.

     We discussed my potential financial interest.  I am paid
on an hourly basis by the agency, and my fees are in no way
contingent on whether the deals are negotiated with the auto
dealers.  The auto dealers are, therefore, not a source of
income to me.  However, we concluded that I could have a
financial interest through the receipt of a "gift".  In other
words, if I purchase an automobile from one of the auto
dealers at a discount of more than $250.00, I will have
received a gift from the auto dealer.  This gift would be
reportable on my Statement of Economic Interest and also
potentially cause me to have to abstain from decision
affecting the auto dealers pursuant to Government Code
Section 87100.

     In our conversation, it appeared that the remedy to this
problem would be to assure that I did not receive any
discount or "gift" in the purchase of the automobile.  In
such instance, I would not have any financial interest.  In
addition, arguably, the "significant segment of the public"
exception would apply in that my interest would be no
different from that of a significant segment of the public.

     I have not quite figured out how to guarantee that the
price I will pay for the automobile will not be any less than
the price which it would be offered to the public generally
since, as we are all aware, there can be a great variation in
the price charged by automobile dealers for their products
depending upon inventories and market conditions.  All things
considered, it would probably be easier for me to make my
purchase in a different community, but I would like to find a
way for my city to receive the sales tax.  At least, having
received the above clarification, I think that I am in a

                                -2-

RUTAN & TUCKER
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS


LUISA MENCHACA, ESQ.
OCTOBER 3, 1990
RE: CONFLICT ISSUES CONCERNING
    ACQUISITION OF AUTOMOBILE


position to discuss this matter with an auto dealer and see
how I can guarantee that I am not receiving any price
discount.

     I recognize that this is not a request for an advisory
letter and that this is a more informal effort to receive
your guidance. I very much appreciate your assistance, and I
think the above guidance will be very helpful to me in trying
to decide how to proceed. If upon further reflection you see
any difficulties with the above or anything which we may have
overlooked, I would very much appreciate your further
instruction.

     Thank you again for your assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        RUTAN & TUCKER

                                        [signature]

                                        David J. Aleshire

DJA:el

5/276/065121-0015/221


                                        -3-
